svg.title: SQL NULL check query explained step by step
svg.desc: A diagram showing how SELECT * FROM retail_sales WHERE column IS NULL works, covering each column checked and why this is done in corporate data workflows

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does SELECT * mean in SQL?')
g.text: Fetch every column and row from the retail_sales table

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the WHERE clause filter rows in SQL?')
g.text: Keep only rows where at least one critical column is missing (NULL)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why use OR instead of AND when checking for NULLs?')
g.text: OR logic — any one NULL triggers the row

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why is sale_date important in retail data?')
sendPrompt('Why is gender checked for NULL in retail analysis?')
sendPrompt('Why is category important in retail sales data?')
sendPrompt('What is COGS in retail and why check it?')
sendPrompt('Why is total_sale a critical field to check?')
sendPrompt('What do you do with NULL rows after finding them?')
g.text: Output — All rows with at least one NULL value
These are dirty / incomplete records to be cleaned or removed

svg.text: Why we use this in corporate data pipelines

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does data quality affect business decisions?')
sendPrompt('What is data cleaning in analytics?')
sendPrompt('How do NULLs affect SQL aggregations like SUM or AVG?')

svg.text: Note: "quantiy" is a typo in your query — correct column name is likely "quantity"
